root_text.sn47.39:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.39:1.7: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.39:1.8: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvanā”ti.

translation_text.sn47.39:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the development of the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.39:1.3: And what is the development of the four kinds of mindfulness meditation?
translation_text.sn47.39:1.4: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.39:1.5: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.39:1.7: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.39:1.8: This is the development of the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.”
